## SECTION 2 — Technology, Science & Space
The massive, highly lucrative world of consumer telecommunications was aggressively shifting toward the deployment of highly advanced, space-based infrastructure. Major aerospace and telecom conglomerates were locked in massive, multi-billion dollar races to launch massive constellations of low-Earth orbit (LEO) satellites. However, the catastrophic, multi-billion dollar bankruptcy of Iridium SSC heavily exposed the massive, incredibly flawed economic logic of these ventures. ## SECTION 3 — Global Economy & Business Shifts
The global financial markets operated in a state of absolute, sustained panic as the brutal, aggressive collapse of the NASDAQ composite index continued unabated. Massive, highly leveraged tech funds aggressively liquidated their holdings, heavily triggering a massive cascade of margin calls across the retail investing sector. The absolute realization that the era of unlimited, cheap capital was over fundamentally shattered the massive, deeply inflated "New Economy" illusion. Investors frantically rotated massive capital back into heavily undervalued "Old Economy" value stocks, actively accelerating the massive dot-com crash.

In the highly competitive global aviation industry, the massive, ongoing rivalry between Boeing and Airbus reached a highly critical juncture. Airbus officially committed to the massive, multi-billion dollar development of the A3XX (which would later become the A380), a massive, double-deck superjumbo jet expressly designed to break Boeing's absolute monopoly on the highly lucrative 747 market. This massive corporate gamble was heavily predicated on the aggressive belief that future global air travel would rely entirely on massive, high-volume hub-to-hub routing.

The Indian political and military establishment aggressively intensified its massive, strategic focus on national security and border integrity. ## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On May 15, 1988, a massive, highly significant geopolitical breakthrough was officially achieved with the aggressive signing of the Geneva Accords. The massive diplomatic agreement, formally guaranteed by the United States and the Soviet Union, explicitly and aggressively mandated the absolute, complete withdrawal of the massive Soviet military forces from Afghanistan. This monumental treaty initiated the end of the devastating, decade-long Soviet-Afghan War, which had fundamentally bankrupted the USSR and heavily catalyzed its impending total collapse.

On May 15, 1940, Richard and Maurice McDonald officially and aggressively opened the very first McDonald's restaurant in San Bernardino, California. Initially a massive, heavily carhop-driven barbecue drive-in, they aggressively revolutionized the global food industry by heavily implementing the "Speedee Service System" in 1948. This incredibly massive innovation applied brutal, factory-line efficiency to food preparation, fundamentally birthing the massive, multi-trillion dollar global fast-food industry that heavily defined late 20th-century consumer culture.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On May 15, 1905, Sukhdev Thapar, the massive, fiercely dedicated, and highly iconic revolutionary of the Indian independence movement, was born in Ludhiana, Punjab. A massive, foundational leader of the Hindustan Socialist Republican Association (HSRA), he aggressively conspired alongside Bhagat Singh and Shivaram Rajguru in the highly publicized Lahore Conspiracy Case. His massive, uncompromising martyrdom via execution by the British authorities firmly cemented him as an absolute, eternal hero of India's armed freedom struggle.

On May 15, 1993, Field Marshal K. M. Cariappa, the massive, deeply revered, and incredibly brilliant military commander, passed away in Bangalore. He achieved absolute, historic immortality by becoming the very first Indian Commander-in-Chief of the Indian Army in 1949, flawlessly taking command from the retreating British forces. He aggressively and brilliantly led the Indian forces on the Western Front during the massive Indo-Pakistani War of 1947, permanently securing his legacy as a massive pillar of the Indian armed forces.
